requirement: Buyer can initiate supplier onboarding process by providing basic information.
Internal approval workflow is required for supplier onboarding process.
Supplier can register with AECI and complete due diligence steps after receiving invitation via email.
Supplier information metadata, process, and approvals are managed in Coupa SIM.
Unvetted suppliers or services go through proper approval channels in Coupa SIM.
Supplier information is accurate and current in Coupa SIM.
Coupa SIM manages supplier creation and onboarding process.
Supplier status management includes purchase block, payment block, supplier deactivation, and reactivation.
Approval workflows involve Procurement, Finance, General Accounting, and Master Data teams.
Internal users can register new suppliers in Coupa for urgent or specific supplier types, adhering to governance and approval controls.
Internal users can update existing supplier information in Coupa, including status changes.
Internal users can extend supplier contracts in Coupa.
Internal users can create one-time vendors in Coupa with a simplified approval flow and reduced data requirements.
Users can view supplier portal status in the Suppliers table.
The supplier onboarding process will be standardized across geographies, with exceptions for Australia in approval workflows and local guidelines for information requirements.
Coupa supports auto-translation of standard information on the user interface for multiple languages, and custom information can be translated by geography's subject matter experts.
